project.name: Quill
project.description: Post-quantum encrypted P2P messenger. Classical asymmetric algorithms (RSA, ECDH) are vulnerable to Shor's algorithm on quantum computers. This project implements a fully quantum-safe communication protocol based on NIST PQC standards (FIPS 203, 204, 205) published in 2024.
project.future_use: Base for engineering thesis on intelligent PQC algorithm selection

priorities.primary: SECURITY — every decision must prioritize cryptographic correctness over convenience
priorities.secondary: CORRECTNESS — working crypto before any UI or optimization
priorities.tertiary: CLARITY — code must be readable and defensible in academic review
anti_goals: Do NOT reimplement crypto primitives — use liboqs and OpenSSL
Do NOT reuse nonces under any circumstance
Do NOT skip signature verification steps
Do NOT use classical asymmetric crypto (RSA, ECDH, DH) anywhere

done: Full PQC Handshake with Server/Client verification
ImGui Frontend (ChatApp, Theme) with Handshake visualization
HKDF-SHA256 session key derivation (Hkdf.h/.cpp): salt = handshake transcript (kyber_pub||ct), domain-separated info, raw secret cleansed after use, RFC 5869 test vector verified

Identity management (IdentityManager.h/.cpp): persistent ML-DSA/Falcon keys, 0600/0700 perms enforced on load (OpenSSH-style), atomic write, sign/verify self-test on load, SHA-3-256 fingerprint (XXXX-XXXX-... format) logged in UI

Profiles + login: ProfileManager (~/.quill/profiles/<name>/ or $QUILL_PROFILES_DIR), profile.json with public fingerprint, name whitelist validation; QID2 at-rest encryption = Argon2id (EVP_KDF, t=3 m=64MiB, params stored in file) + AES-256-GCM; wrong passphrase = GCM tag fail (single indistinguishable error); QID1->QID2 in-place migration; empty passphrase = plaintext QID1 with UI warning; ImGui login screen, logout disabled during active session (keys needed for PFS rotation), OPENSSL_cleanse on logout/shutdown

TOFU (TrustStore.h/.cpp): known_hosts in profile dir (JSON, 0600, atomic write), states UNVERIFIED/KNOWN/VERIFIED/MISMATCH, comparison by full SHA-3-256 (short fingerprint is display-only); client keys peers by srv:host:port (full key-change detection, fail-closed BEFORE KEM encaps), server recognizes returning clients by key (cli:fingerprint); MISMATCH never overwrites the stored entry (explicit Remove required); corrupted known_hosts = hard error; UI: PEER TRUST section in handshake panel + MARK AS VERIFIED, Tools > Trusted Peers window (Verify/Remove)

CryptoManager: full PQC handshake (client+server), TOFU, HKDF and identity in one UI-free layer (quill_core static lib); ChatApp::do_*_handshake are thin wrappers (logs + visualizer via StepCallback); TofuMismatchError carries expected/received fingerprints; security invariants (verify before encaps/decaps, fail-closed TOFU, secret cleanse) enforced in the crypto layer

Google Test suite (tests/, 81 tests): RFC 5869 vectors, Argon2, AES-GCM tamper, Kyber+Dilithium all levels, identity (QID1/QID2, wrong pass, migration, perms, corruption), profiles (path traversal), TrustStore (full TOFU cycle + key-change attack), MessageFormat, FileTransfer (corrupted/missing chunk, bad hash), integration handshake over socketpair incl. MITM-block and tampered-ciphertext rejection
QID1 hardening: SHA-3-256 checksum appended to plaintext key files (deterministic corruption detection; sign/verify self-test alone did not catch every byte flip in ML-DSA sk)
File transfer: 64KB chunking, AES-256-GCM per chunk, SHA-3-256 integrity check (FileSender/FileReceiver)
File transfer UI: file picker (portable-file-dialogs), progress bars, download directory
not_done: File transfer improvements: selective repeat retransmission, per-chunk SHA-3
NAT Traversal: UDP hole punching + rendezvous server
Replay protection at the chat-message level (no sequence numbers in CHAT messages)
current_state.note: Everything declared as DONE in Quill_Prezentacja.pptx now exists in code (file transfer, identity, TOFU, HKDF, CryptoManager, unit tests). Remaining items above were marked as 'planned' on the slides.

session_encryption.key_derivation: HKDF-SHA256(ikm=Kyber shared_secret, salt=kyber_pub||kem_ciphertext, info='quill-aes256gcm-session-v1|LEVEL') -> 32B AES key; raw secret cleansed after derivation
session_encryption.nonce: 12 bytes, random per message via RAND_bytes — NEVER reuse
session_encryption.tag: 16 bytes GCM auth tag appended to ciphertext

critical_rules: Never reuse a nonce with the same key
Always verify PQC signatures before trusting received KEM material
Never fall back to classical crypto

protocol.transport: TCP with length-prefixed framing (uint32_t network byte order)

message_format.encoding: JSON via nlohmann/json (using binary_t for raw bytes)

architecture.concurrency: std::thread per client, std::mutex for shared state (e.g., m_clients_mtx, m_rooms_mtx)

src/frontend/.description: ImGui based UI, Chat application logic, and rendering

src/network/.description: TCP Sockets, Client and Server object-oriented wrappers

src/protocol/.description: JSON message formatting, parsing and file transfer

directory_tree.tests/: Google Test suite (81 tests) — linked against quill_core static lib (crypto+network+protocol, no UI)

ai_assistant_instructions.language: Respond in Polish unless asked otherwise
ai_assistant_instructions.focus: Security correctness above all else — always flag any crypto misuse
ai_assistant_instructions.style: Prefer clear, readable code over clever one-liners — this is academic code
ai_assistant_instructions.libs: Use liboqs for all PQC, OpenSSL for AES/SHA/RAND — do not suggest other crypto libs
ai_assistant_instructions.context_awareness: Always refer to 'architecture.directory_tree' when suggesting where to place new code or refactor existing ones.
